title: Glechoma pungens
intro: Glechoma pungens, also known as Russian sage, is a perennial plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The health benefits of Glechoma pungens include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to alleviate digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Glechoma pungens is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Glechoma pungens is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. This plant is native to the mountains of East Asia. It is an herbaceous perennial with fragrant leaves and purple flowers. Glechoma pungens has been classified as a member of the genus Glechoma, which is further divided into the family Lamiaceae and the order Lamiales. Within its taxonomic classification, Glechoma pungens is also referred to as a type of hardy groundcover.

It is believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs.
